diagnosis.differential_diagnosis: COPD exacerbation, Anemia, Diastolic heart failure, Atrial fibrillation, Constipation, Acute kidney injury
diagnosis.diagnostic_reasoning: The patient presented with bilateral lower extremity erythema/swelling and worsening shortness of breath over the past 5 days, alongside a significant drop in hematocrit. Physical exam findings revealed diffuse wheezes, prolonged systolic murmur, and echocardiography showed severe aortic stenosis along with other cardiac abnormalities. Elevated BNP and improvements post-diuretic treatment also supported the diagnosis.
